Each novel Alex has read has provided a unique experience, helping to broaden their understanding of human connections. The first novel Alex read was relatively short, containing 120 pages. It took Alex four days to finish, which suggests a consistent reading pace. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages, and it took Alex six days to complete. This indicates that Alex read at the same rate each day. The third novel was the longest, with 240 pages, and it took Alex eight days to finish. This pattern of reading time and pages aligns with a steady pace. To calculate the average number of pages Alex read per day, we need to determine the total number of pages across all three novels. Adding the pages from each novel gives a total of 120 plus 180 plus 240. This results in a total of 540 pages. The total number of days Alex spent reading all three novels is four plus six plus eight. This adds up to 18 days in total. Dividing the total pages by the total days will give the average number of pages read per day. 540 divided by 18 equals 30. This means Alex read an average of 30 pages per day across all three novels. The consistency in reading pace is evident in the way each novel was completed. Alex maintained the same number of pages read each day, which is why the average is uniform. This uniformity in reading habits helps in tracking progress and setting goals. The third novel, with 240 pages, followed the same pattern. Each novel was read at the same pace, which is a testament to Alex’s discipline. The average of 30 pages per day is a reflection of this consistency. It is important to note that the number of pages and the number of days are directly proportional.
